step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the majority view — option C — that a habit developed under psychological cause is best described as useful habits. However, I offer a nuanced perspective: while many psychologically-driven habits initially seem functional or beneficial (providing short-term emotional relief), they often lose utility over time and transition into maladaptive patterns. For instance, avoiding social interaction due to low confidence may appear "useful" immediately—it offers comfort—but eventually undermines personal growth and relationships. Thus, labeling such habits as “useful” risks oversimplifying their complex role in emotional well-being. Still, given the emphasis on functionality within psychological frameworks, C remains the most aligned with current understanding. Final
